---
title: "Chapter 7 - Jnana-Vijnana Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">ज्ञानविज्ञानयोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-06.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-08.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-7-1" data-reference="7.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-1">7.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">With your mind clinging to me, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, practicing <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> and taking refuge in me, hear how you shall know me fully and beyond doubt.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मय्यासक्तमनाः पार्थ योगं युञ्जन्मदाश्रयः ।<br>
असंशयं समग्रं मां यथा ज्ञास्यसि तच्छृणु ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-2" data-reference="7.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-2">7.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I shall declare to you in full this knowledge together with its realization; once you know it, nothing further remains to be known here.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ज्ञानं तेऽहं सविज्ञानमिदं वक्ष्याम्यशेषतः ।<br>
यज्ज्ञात्वा नेह भूयोऽन्यज्ज्ञातव्यमवशिष्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-3" data-reference="7.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-3">7.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Among thousands of people, scarcely one strives for perfection; and of those who strive and succeed, scarcely one knows me in truth.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मनुष्याणां सहस्रेषु कश्चिद्यतति सिद्धये ।<br>
यततामपि सिद्धानां कश्चिन्मां वेत्ति तत्त्वतः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-4" data-reference="7.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-4">7.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, understanding, and ego—so is my <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">prakriti</span> divided eightfold.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">भूमिरापोऽनलो वायुः खं मनो बुद्धिरेव च ।<br>
अहङ्कार इतीयं मे भिन्ना प्रकृतिरष्टधा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-5" data-reference="7.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-5">7.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">This is my lower nature; but know my other nature, the higher, O mighty-armed one—the living principle by which this world is sustained.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अपरेयमितस्त्वन्यां प्रकृतिं विद्धि मे पराम् ।<br>
जीवभूतां महाबाहो ययेदं धार्यते जगत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-6" data-reference="7.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-6">7.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Know that all beings have their womb in these; I am the origin of the whole universe, and its dissolution as well.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एतद्योनीनि भूतानि सर्वाणीत्युपधारय ।<br>
अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगतः प्रभवः प्रलयस्तथा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-7" data-reference="7.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-7">7.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">There is nothing higher than me, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." aria-label="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." title="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." data-glossary-id="dhananjaya">Dhananjaya</span>; on me all this is strung, like rows of gems upon a thread.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मत्तः परतरं नान्यत्किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय ।<br>
मयि सर्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-8" data-reference="7.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-8">7.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I am the taste in water, O son of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kunti</span>, the light in moon and sun, the syllable <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." aria-label="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." title="Om: Sacred syllable used as a concise sign of the absolute or divine." data-glossary-id="om">Om</span> in all the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." aria-label="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." title="Vedas: Ancient sacred texts of the Vedic tradition." data-glossary-id="veda">Vedas</span>, the sound in space, the manhood in men.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">रसोऽहमप्सु कौन्तेय प्रभास्मि शशिसूर्ययोः ।<br>
प्रणवः सर्ववेदेषु शब्दः खे पौरुषं नृषु ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-9" data-reference="7.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-9">7.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I am the pure fragrance in the earth and the brightness in fire;<br>
I am the life in all beings and the austerity in ascetics.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पुण्यो गन्धः पृथिव्यां च तेजश्चास्मि विभावसौ ।<br>
जीवनं सर्वभूतेषु तपश्चास्मि तपस्विषु ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-10" data-reference="7.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-10">7.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Know me, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, as the eternal seed of all beings; I am the understanding of the wise, the splendor of the splendid.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम् ।<br>
बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-11" data-reference="7.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-11">7.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I am the strength of the strong, free of desire and passion; in beings I am desire that does not conflict with <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span>, O chief of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharatas</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">बलं बलवतां चाहं कामरागविवर्जितम् ।<br>
धर्माविरुद्धो भूतेषु कामोऽस्मि भरतर्षभ ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-12" data-reference="7.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-12">7.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whatever states there are—of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattva</span>, of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajas</span>, or of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamas</span>—know them all to come from me; yet I am not in them, but they are in me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ये चैव सात्त्विका भावा राजसास्तामसाश्च ये ।<br>
मत्त एवेति तान्विद्धि न त्वहं तेषु ते मयि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-13" data-reference="7.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-13">7.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Deluded by these three states made of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>, this whole world does not know me, who am above them and imperishable.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">त्रिभिर्गुणमयैर्भावैरेभिः सर्वमिदं जगत् ।<br>
मोहितं नाभिजानाति मामेभ्यः परमव्ययम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-14" data-reference="7.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-14">7.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For this divine <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="maya: Power of appearance, concealment, or manifestation; context-dependent." aria-label="maya: Power of appearance, concealment, or manifestation; context-dependent." title="maya: Power of appearance, concealment, or manifestation; context-dependent." data-glossary-id="maya">maya</span> of mine, made of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>, is hard to cross; but those who take refuge in me alone pass beyond this <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="maya: Power of appearance, concealment, or manifestation; context-dependent." aria-label="maya: Power of appearance, concealment, or manifestation; context-dependent." title="maya: Power of appearance, concealment, or manifestation; context-dependent." data-glossary-id="maya">maya</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">दैवी ह्येषा गुणमयी मम माया दुरत्यया ।<br>
मामेव ये प्रपद्यन्ते मायामेतां तरन्ति ते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-15" data-reference="7.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-15">7.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The evildoers, the deluded, the lowest of men, their knowledge stripped away by <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="maya: Power of appearance, concealment, or manifestation; context-dependent." aria-label="maya: Power of appearance, concealment, or manifestation; context-dependent." title="maya: Power of appearance, concealment, or manifestation; context-dependent." data-glossary-id="maya">maya</span>, given over to a demonic nature, do not take refuge in me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न मां दुष्कृतिनो मूढाः प्रपद्यन्ते नराधमाः ।<br>
माययापहृतज्ञाना आसुरं भावमाश्रिताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-16" data-reference="7.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-16">7.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Four kinds of those who do good worship me, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>: the distressed, the seeker of knowledge, the seeker of gain, and the wise, O best of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharatas</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">चतुर्विधा भजन्ते मां जनाः सुकृतिनोऽर्जुन ।<br>
आर्तो जिज्ञासुरर्थार्थी ज्ञानी च भरतर्षभ ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-17" data-reference="7.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-17">7.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Of these, the wise one, ever disciplined, devoted to the one alone, stands highest; for to the wise I am exceedingly dear, and he is dear to me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तेषां ज्ञानी नित्ययुक्त एकभक्तिर्विशिष्यते ।<br>
प्रियो हि ज्ञानिनोऽत्यर्थमहं स च मम प्रियः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-18" data-reference="7.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-18">7.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">All of these are noble, but the wise one I hold to be my very self;<br>
for, steady in himself, he is set on me alone as the highest goal.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">उदाराः सर्व एवैते ज्ञानी त्वात्मैव मे मतम् ।<br>
आस्थितः स हि युक्तात्मा मामेवानुत्तमां गतिम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-19" data-reference="7.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-19">7.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">At the end of many births the wise one comes to me, knowing that <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vasudeva: Name or epithet of Krishna, son of Vasudeva." aria-label="Vasudeva: Name or epithet of Krishna, son of Vasudeva." title="Vasudeva: Name or epithet of Krishna, son of Vasudeva." data-glossary-id="vasudeva">Vasudeva</span> is all; such a great soul is very rare.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">बहूनां जन्मनामन्ते ज्ञानवान्मां प्रपद्यते ।<br>
वासुदेवः सर्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-20" data-reference="7.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-20">7.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those whose knowledge is carried off by this or that desire take refuge in other gods, following this and that observance, constrained by their own nature.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कामैस्तैस्तैर्हृतज्ञानाः प्रपद्यन्तेऽन्यदेवताः ।<br>
तं तं नियममास्थाय प्रकृत्या नियताः स्वया ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-21" data-reference="7.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-21">7.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whatever form a devotee seeks to worship with faith, it is I who make that faith of his steady.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यो यो यां यां तनुं भक्तः श्रद्धयार्चितुमिच्छति ।<br>
तस्य तस्याचलां श्रद्धां तामेव विदधाम्यहम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-22" data-reference="7.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-22">7.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Yoked with that faith, he seeks to honor that form, and from it obtains his desires—though it is I alone who grant them.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">स तया श्रद्धया युक्तस्तस्याराधनमीहते ।<br>
लभते च ततः कामान्मयैव विहितान्हि तान् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-23" data-reference="7.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-23">7.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But the fruit won by these small-minded ones is finite; those who worship the gods go to the gods, while those devoted to me come to me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अन्तवत्तु फलं तेषां तद्भवत्यल्पमेधसाम् ।<br>
देवान्देवयजो यान्ति मद्भक्ता यान्ति मामपि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-24" data-reference="7.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-24">7.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The unwise take me, the unmanifest, to have come into manifest being, not knowing my higher nature, imperishable and supreme.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अव्यक्तं व्यक्तिमापन्नं मन्यन्ते मामबुद्धयः ।<br>
परं भावमजानन्तो ममाव्ययमनुत्तमम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-25" data-reference="7.25">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-25">7.25</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Veiled by my <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="maya: Power of appearance, concealment, or manifestation; context-dependent." aria-label="maya: Power of appearance, concealment, or manifestation; context-dependent." title="maya: Power of appearance, concealment, or manifestation; context-dependent." data-glossary-id="maya">yoga-maya</span>, I am not manifest to all; this deluded world does not know me, the unborn and imperishable.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नाहं प्रकाशः सर्वस्य योगमायासमावृतः ।<br>
मूढोऽयं नाभिजानाति लोको मामजमव्ययम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-26" data-reference="7.26">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-26">7.26</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I know the beings that are past, that are present, and that are to come, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>; but no one knows me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">वेदाहं समतीतानि वर्तमानानि चार्जुन ।<br>
भविष्याणि च भूतानि मां तु वेद न कश्चन ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-27" data-reference="7.27">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-27">7.27</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">By the delusion of the pairs of opposites, sprung from desire and aversion, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>, all beings fall into bewilderment at birth, O scorcher of foes.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इच्छाद्वेषसमुत्थेन द्वन्द्वमोहेन भारत ।<br>
सर्वभूतानि सम्मोहं सर्गे यान्ति परन्तप ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-28" data-reference="7.28">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-28">7.28</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But those of pure deeds, whose sins have ended, freed from the delusion of the pairs of opposites, worship me with firm resolve.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">येषां त्वन्तगतं पापं जनानां पुण्यकर्मणाम् ।<br>
ते द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्मुक्ता भजन्ते मां दृढव्रताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-29" data-reference="7.29">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-29">7.29</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those who, taking refuge in me, strive for release from old age and death know that <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>, the whole adhyatma, and the whole of action.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">जरामरणमोक्षाय मामाश्रित्य यतन्ति ये ।<br>
ते ब्रह्म तद्विदुः कृत्स्नमध्यात्मं कर्म चाखिलम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-7-30" data-reference="7.30">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-7-30">7.30</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those who know me together with the adhibhuta, the adhidaiva, and the adhiyajna—they, their minds yoked, know me even at the hour of death.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">साधिभूताधिदैवं मां साधियज्ञं च ये विदुः ।<br>
प्रयाणकालेऽपि च मां ते विदुर्युक्तचेतसः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
